**Minerals and Rocks**  
**mineral** \- naturally occurring, inorganic, solid with crystal structure and definite chemical composition  
\*naturally occurring=made by Earth \*inorganic=not living matter \*solid state of matter  
\*definite chemical composition=made of only certain atoms  
\*crystal \- regular repeating pattern of atoms  
ex.  halite is cubic, NaCl (sodium chloride)  
![][image1]

**To identify a mineral, a minimum of 3 property tests are done.**

**color** \- some minerals have a very distinct color (sulfur is yellow)  
\-some minerals are the same color (quartz,halite,gypsum,calcite are clear/white)  
\-some minerals are many colors (quartz=clear,white,pink,brown,blue,purple)

**luster** \- way a mineral’s surface reflects light  
\-metallic \= shiny, metal-like (pyrite,galena,magnetite,graphite)  
\-nonmetallic \= glassy(quartz,halite),pearly(calcite,gypsum),waxy(sulfur,feldspar,talc)  
\-hematite can be metallic(silver) or nonmetallic(dull red/brown)

**streak** \- powder form of a mineral  
\-test for by rubbing mineral across a streak plate (unglazed porcelain)  
\-streak doesn’t always match color of mineral (hematite,pyrite)

**hardness** \- minerals ability to resist being scratched  
\-Mohs Hardness Scale 1-10, 1 is softest(talc) and 10 is hardest(diamond)  
\-use fingernail(2.5), copper plate(3), glass plate(6) to scratch

**cleavage** \- mineral breaks into flat sheets (mica,gypsum,calcite,galena,halite,feldspar)

**fracture** \- mineral breaks into jagged pieces (quartz,sulfur,pyrite,magnetite,hematite)

**unique properties**  
\-calcite fizzes with hydrochloric acid	\-magnetite is magnetic  
\-calcite produces double image		\-fluorite glows in black light
